decision: make Promise.then, Promise.catch, and Promise.finally reporting labels visible as recall-loss admission_rejections without opening exact continuation admission

surfaces.note: No separate finally row was observed in the v1 JS/TS audit, but focused report rows now keep future sightings visible.

implemented_reporting.strict_exact_change: JS-like then/catch/finally selector calls stay closed in strict-exact unless a future proof-backed continuation capability explicitly admits them. The interpreter still treats simple parameter-callback fixtures as opaque symbolic calls, so recall-loss reports can now count the missing evidence.

current_crates_report_summary.note: The repository-local crates surface has no Promise continuation source rows; focused JS fixtures now provide the regression signal.

next_recovery_priority.candidate: PromiseLike receiver producer proof for first-party then/catch chains
next_recovery_priority.quant_signal: 68 unhinted receiver occurrences across then (36) and catch (32), plus 4 array-literal-binding receiver hints and 1 promise-constructor-binding hint
next_recovery_priority.why_first: Continuation recovery cannot be safe until the receiver is proven PromiseLike rather than an arbitrary selector-only method.

next_recovery_priority.candidate: Promise rejection/settlement channel proof for Promise.reject and catch/finally
next_recovery_priority.quant_signal: 33 Promise.reject occurrences and 34 catch occurrences in the JS/TS audit
next_recovery_priority.why_second: Rejection and settlement channels must stay distinct from synchronous values and fulfillment-only continuations.

next_recovery_priority.quant_signal: 39 then occurrences, but only after receiver, fulfillment, rejection, and callback obligations are dependency-closed
next_recovery_priority.why_third: The value graph can model proven Promise.resolve(...).then(...) payloads, but exact admission needs all continuation obligations proven first.

next_work: audit the 68 unhinted then/catch receiver occurrences by repo and source shape
add PromiseLike receiver producer diagnostics that distinguish Promise.resolve results, async function returns, constructor-created promises, and unknown thenables
keep exact continuation admission closed until the receiver producer proof and rejection/callback obligations have hard negatives
